name: KSHV_LANA_tandem_repeat
description: KSHV LANA (ORF73) central repeat region: a low-complexity internal domain of ~1.6 kb encoding tandem Glu/Gln/Asp/Pro-rich repeats and a long acidic stretch. Repeat unit lengths and copy numbers vary between KSHV strains and are a clonality / strain-typing marker. The LANA C-terminal DNA-binding domain (DBD) binds the LANA-binding sites (LBS1/LBS2) in the TR, tethering the episome to host chromatin; the central repeat is dispensable for tethering but modulates LANA's interaction with histones and the SUV39H1 complex. Short reads pile up massively over the repeat — long-read or assembly-based length estimation is required.
expected_copy_count: strain-variable; ~21-23 leucine-zipper-like Q-rich repeats in BC-1 reference
